title: Lost in Translation
author: Sofia Coppola
snippet: A jet-lagged Tokyo hotel and a quiet friendship. Made for a single viewer with the lights low.
full_text:
Two lost souls visiting Tokyo -- the young, neglected wife of a photographer and a washed-up movie star shooting a TV commercial -- find an odd solace and pensive freedom to be real in each other's company, away from their lives in America.

Watch on: Rent: Amazon Video, Google Play Movies
watch_on: Rent: Amazon Video, Google Play Movies

title: Her
author: Spike Jonze
snippet: Spike Jonze's loneliest film. The kind of movie that feels different watched alone.
full_text:
In the not so distant future, Theodore, a lonely writer, purchases a newly developed operating system designed to meet the user's every need. To Theodore's surprise, a romantic relationship develops between him and his operating system. This unconventional love story blends science fiction and romance in a sweet tale that explores the nature of love and the ways that technology isolates and connects us all.

Watch on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store
watch_on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store

title: In the Mood for Love
author: Wong Kar-Wai
snippet: Wong Kar-wai. Two neighbors, a hallway, every glance held a beat too long.
full_text:
In 1960s Hong Kong, two neighbors form an intimate bond after making a discovery about their spouses in this visually stunning tale of unrequited love.

Watch on: HBO Max, HBO Max Amazon Channel, Criterion Channel
tagline: Feel the heat, keep the feeling burning, let the sensation explode.
watch_on: HBO Max, HBO Max Amazon Channel, Criterion Channel

title: Lawrence of Arabia
author: David Lean
snippet: Nearly four hours and not a single dragging minute. Watch widescreen, with no one talking.
full_text:
During World War I, English officer Thomas Edward 'T.E.' Lawrence sets out to unite and lead the diverse, often warring, Arab tribes to fight the Turks.

Watch on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store
watch_on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store

title: Wings of Desire
author: Wim Wenders
snippet: Wenders. Black-and-white angels listening to a city. Slow but never boring.
full_text:
Two angels, Damiel and Cassiel, glide through the streets of Berlin, observing the bustling population, providing invisible rays of hope to the distressed but never interacting with them. When Damiel falls in love with lonely trapeze artist Marion, the angel longs to experience life in the physical world, and finds — with some words of wisdom from actor Peter Falk — that it might be possible for him to take human form.

Watch on: HBO Max, HBO Max Amazon Channel, Criterion Channel
tagline: There are angels on the streets of Berlin.
watch_on: HBO Max, HBO Max Amazon Channel, Criterion Channel

title: Paterson
author: Jim Jarmusch
snippet: A bus driver writes poetry. Almost nothing happens. That's the point.
full_text:
A week in the life of Paterson, a poet bus driver, and his wife Laura, a very creative artist, who live in Paterson, New Jersey, hometown of many famous poets and artists.

Watch on: Amazon Prime Video, Amazon Prime Video with Ads
tagline: Beauty is often found in the smallest details.
watch_on: Amazon Prime Video, Amazon Prime Video with Ads

title: Past Lives
author: Celine Song
snippet: Three timelines, one love story. New canon, won't disappoint anyone who liked Lost in Translation.
full_text:
After decades apart, childhood friends Nora and Hae Sung are reunited in New York for one fateful weekend as they confront notions of destiny, love, and the choices that make a life.

Watch on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store
watch_on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store

title: Drive My Car
author: Ryusuke Hamaguchi
snippet: Three-hour quiet masterpiece. Long drives, Chekhov, grief. The ending repays the runtime.
full_text:
Yusuke Kafuku, a stage actor and director, still unable, after two years, to cope with the loss of his beloved wife, accepts to direct Uncle Vanya at a theater festival in Hiroshima. There he meets Misaki, an introverted young woman, appointed to drive his car. In between rides, secrets from the past and heartfelt confessions will be unveiled.

Watch on: Criterion Channel

title: Moonlight
author: Barry Jenkins
snippet: Three chapters, one life. Aesthetic precision, emotional payoff.
full_text:
The tender, heartbreaking story of a young man’s struggle to find himself, told across three defining chapters in his life as he experiences the ecstasy, pain, and beauty of falling in love, while grappling with his own sexuality.

Watch on: HBO Max
tagline: This is the story of a lifetime

title: The Tree of Life
author: Terrence Malick
snippet: Malick at his most ambitious. Cosmos to suburban Texas. Either it works on you or it doesn't.
full_text:
The impressionistic story of a Texas family in the 1950s. The film follows the life journey of the eldest son, Jack, through the innocence of childhood to his disillusioned adult years as he tries to reconcile a complicated relationship with his father. Jack finds himself a lost soul in the modern world, seeking answers to the origins and meaning of life while questioning the existence of faith.
